title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, Delivery Limits, and Parking · Contract Lens: Dlx Policy Permission
summary: A queue hardcodes a missing DLX, cycles messages indefinitely, drops quorum poison messages after the default delivery limit, and treats a dead-letter queue as an unowned archive. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dlx policy permission increment in dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.
Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Dlx Policy Permission · Theory Dlx Policy Permission
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6920 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to route exhausted poison messages through a bound, owned, and retained remediation path; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.
